title: Isoperimetry, Spectral Geometry and Stability of Soap-Bubbles
speaker: Emanuel Milman
abstract:
Simonyi 101
We develop the spectral analysis of the Jacobi operator on the interfaces of soap-bubble clusters. By Plateau's laws, these always meet in threes at 
120
∘
-angles, and thus naturally interact via 3 linearly independent "conformal" boundary conditions (a mixture of Dirichlet and Robin). This gives rise to a self-adjoint operator, whose spectral properties determine the \emph{stability} of the soap-bubbles -- whether an infinitesimal regular perturbation preserving volume to first order yields a non-negative second variation of area modulo the volume constraint. In essence, stability is an infinitesimal notion of local isoperimetry, amounting to verifying a Poincar\'e-type inequality on soap-bubble clusters.We verify that for all 
n≥3
 and 
2≤k≤n+1
, the standard 
k
-bubble clusters in 
n
-dimensional Euclidean, spherical and hyperbolic spaces, which are conjectured to be global isoperimetric minimizers of total perimeter, are indeed stable. In fact, stability holds for all M\"obius-flat \emph{partitions}, in which several cells are allowed to have infinite volume. In the Gaussian setting (when volume and area are weighted relative to the Gaussian density), any partition obeying Plateau's laws and whose interfaces are all \emph{flat}, is stable. Our proof relies on a new conjugated Brascamp-Lieb inequality on partitions with conformally flat umbilical boundary, and the construction of a good conformally flattening boundary potential.

proof_stages.label: Stability problem and main results
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates soap-bubble clusters, introduces stability as nonnegativity of the second variation under first-order volume constraints, and states stability results for standard bubbles, Möbius-flat partitions, and flat Gaussian partitions.

proof_stages.label: Weighted partitions and standard-bubble framework
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formalizes weighted volume, weighted area, partitions, clusters, interfaces, and global isoperimetric minimizers, then reviews Sullivan’s standard-bubble construction and the known status of the corresponding minimizing conjectures.

proof_stages.label: Regular stationary clusters and Jacobi second variation
proof_stages.summary: Regularity decomposes the boundary into smooth interfaces, triple sets, and higher singular strata; stationarity gives CMC/Young-Laplace and 120-degree Plateau laws; the second variation becomes a Jacobi quadratic form with volume, Kirchhoff, and conformal boundary conditions.

proof_stages.label: Conformal reduction to a Poincare inequality
proof_stages.summary: After integration by parts with a positive auxiliary potential, the stability quadratic form is reduced—when the potential conformally flattens the interface boundaries—to a weighted Poincare-type inequality, with the flat Gaussian case giving the sharp Gaussian Poincare target.

proof_stages.label: Multi-bubble conjugated Brascamp-Lieb inequality
proof_stages.summary: Classical and conjugated Brascamp-Lieb inequalities are adapted to the multi-bubble setting via a Bochner identity and dualization, with the main new issue being cancellation of boundary-of-boundary terms at tetrahedral singularities.

proof_stages.label: Application to Gaussian and Möbius-flat model spaces
proof_stages.summary: For Gaussian flat partitions one takes the potential to be one, while for Möbius-flat partitions in constant-curvature model spaces a special positive potential is constructed and inserted into the Brascamp-Lieb inequality to prove stability.
